meta_title: For Students: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Mastering API Integration: Debugging for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with API errors. Learn advanced debugging techniques for AI builders using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development.
content.answer_block: Debugging API integrations requires moving beyond trial and error. By leveraging Claude Code for iterative refinement and Antigravity for architectural visualization, you can isolate network failures, validate payloads, and identify logic bottlenecks in minutes. Stop guessing; start tracing your requests through the full stack to resolve integration failures permanently.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks. Your terminal is a graveyard of 500-level errors and cryptic JSON responses. You’ve spent hours refactoring, yet the API integration remains a black box of frustration. You feel the weight of a project stalling, the gap between your vision and the reality of broken code widening with every failed request.

But here is the truth: debugging isn't a test of your intelligence; it is a test of your toolkit. By shifting from manual logging to systematic, AI-assisted observability, you transform your debugging process from a source of anxiety into a technical superpower. It is time to stop wrestling with your code and start commanding it.

steps.title: Isolate the Request Payload
steps.description: Before debugging the logic, ensure the data structure matches the API schema. Use Claude Code to map your local objects to the API's expected JSON format.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this API request payload against the provided OpenAPI spec. Identify missing fields or incorrect data types.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map local data model to API schema'

steps.title: Implement Request Tracing
steps.description: Visibility is the cure for ambiguity. Wrap your API calls in middleware that logs the full request/response cycle for instant identification of serialization errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust middleware wrapper for Axios/Fetch that logs headers, payloads, and timing for every API request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/api/client.ts --instruction 'insert request logging middleware'

steps.title: Validate Auth Handshakes
steps.description: Most integration failures stem from malformed headers. We will use Claude Code to verify token injection and environment variable resolution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the authentication header construction logic. Ensure Bearer tokens are injected correctly from process.env.

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases
steps.description: Don't just test success. Use Antigravity to simulate rate limits, timeouts, and malformed responses to ensure your error handling is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock response generator that simulates 429 rate limit errors and 503 gateway timeouts for my current API integration.

steps.title: Automate Regression Testing
steps.description: Lock in your fix. Once the API is stable, use Claude Code to generate unit tests that ensure future refactors don't break the connection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write comprehensive Jest unit tests for this API integration covering success, failure, and timeout states.

pro_tips: Always log the raw HTTP response body before parsing, as API providers often return HTML error pages during outages.
Use a local proxy like Charles or Proxyman to inspect requests if your production environment obscures logs.
Never rely on 'try-catch' blocks alone; implement a circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures in your app.
Version your API calls in your code base; hardcoding endpoints without versioning is a recipe for silent breaking changes.
Use Claude Code to document your integration flow; if you can't explain it to the AI, you don't understand the API logic well enough.
content.next_steps: After mastering these debugging techniques, integrate these API calls into a persistent database, then move on to building a frontend dashboard to visualize your data streams.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to stop debugging and start building. Don't let your project stall at the API layer; harness these tools to push your app into production. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
